Threads_engage_text: When people say Bitcoin lets you be your own bank, they mean this: with Bitcoin stored in your own wallet, you are the only person who can authorise a transaction. Not a bank manager. Not a government. Not a payment processor. You.
Main_Alt_1: When people say Bitcoin lets you be your own bank, they mean this: with Bitcoin stored in your own wallet, you are the only person who can authorise a transaction.
Main_Alt_2: Not a bank manager. Not a government. Not a payment processor. You. Your wallet is controlled by a private key — a unique string of letters and numbers that only you hold.
Main_Alt_3: Whoever has that key controls the Bitcoin. No key, no access. Your key, your Bitcoin.
Main_Alt_4: It's the digital equivalent of gold bars in your own safe. The responsibility that comes with this is real.
R1_Alt_1: If you lose your private key and have not backed it up, nobody can help you recover your funds — there's no customer service line for the Bitcoin network.
R1_Alt_2: This is why understanding how to store your key safely is the most important practical skill in Bitcoin.
R1_Alt_3: A hardware wallet — a small physical device that stores your key offline — is the standard solution.
R1_Alt_4: A written backup of your 12 or 24 seed words, stored somewhere safe and private, is your insurance policy. It sounds technical but takes about 30 minutes to set up properly.
